mentions.note: Cramer congratulated the caller on the gain, describing the position as effectively resolved via the acquisition bid.
Same 'source of funds' dynamic as Nvidia; business is humming, own it, don't trade it.
Preferred over Lithium Americas as the safer way to play lithium, even after a big run.
Battery maker for drones/satellites; an interesting speculative name that has pulled back to a good entry.
Down 23% over the past year, but Cramer is a buyer, not a seller, citing confidence in CEO Rene Haas.
Consistently good with few surprises; expects a solid growth quarter that helps drive the stock over time.
Largest asset manager on earth, pushing new fund categories; expects a pretty good number.
Gone from ugly duckling to beautiful swan; analysts haven't caught up, so upside estimate revisions keep driving the stock higher.
Well-run, high-quality growth name at just 13x earnings even after a strong week — likely to make a run at all-time highs.
Trades below 12x next year's earnings despite an ~80% return last year; improving Medicare Advantage rates and stabilizing retail pharmacy support further re-rating.
Weakness reflects overdone China-tariff concerns; hold for now, with a Supreme Court tariff win worth ~25 points of upside.
Named as the preferred logistics name over Radiant Logistics.
Raj Subramaniam has done a fantastic job; calls it a winner.
Not good enough a story to justify 16x earnings, and Cramer won't tolerate the caller's margin position — told him to cut losses.
Takeover/advisory business should be tremendous; expects a number increase.
Doesn't typically recommend truckers, but a good number here would embolden further trucking exposure.
Worried about Jamie Dimon's cautious conference-call tone hitting the stock even if the quarter is strong; wait for a dip on the call, then buy.
Weakness tied more to the Kenvue acquisition overhang than the core business; intriguing at 13x earnings with a 5% yield.
Cramer redirected the caller away from this name entirely in favor of Albemarle.
Asset-gathering has been extraordinary under Ted Pick; expects excellent numbers.
Caller down 20% from the highs; thesis unchanged as long as execution continues — buy a little more, especially into further weakness.
Weakness is money managers using it as a 'source of funds' to fund new positions, not a fundamentals issue; own it, don't trade it.
Up-and-down performer lately; Cramer prefers other bank stocks heading into earnings.
Redirected the caller to FedEx instead as the better way to play logistics.
Can't tolerate this kind of enterprise software name right now.
Beat-and-raise machine overcoming tariff pressure via cost cuts; a steal at 20x earnings with potential tariff-relief upside.
Down but only ~1% for the year; Cramer would be a buyer here, not a seller.
Expects a monster quarter that could help shake off selling pressure in partner Nvidia's stock.
Turnaround under new CEO accelerating after a blowout quarter; still a buy for new money despite the run, a hold for October buyers.
Held for the Charitable Trust; CEO Charlie Sharf has pivoted the bank toward higher-margin corporate/M&A business with strong cost control.
